text:
I'll search for this paper to verify whether it exists.
The search results show clear evidence of this paper. I found multiple references to it across reliable sources.

From the search results, I can confirm:

1. 
The paper "A Hierarchy of Graphical Models for Counterfactual Inferences" by H. Yang and E. Bareinboim is confirmed as a NeurIPS-25 paper in the Proceedings of the 39th Annual Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (Columbia CausalAI Laboratory, Technical Report R-130, May 2025)
.

2. 
The OpenReview PDF shows the paper with authors Hongshuo Yang and text confirming this is the correct work
.

3. The paper has a dedicated PDF hosted at causalai.net (https://causalai.net/r130.pdf) and is also available through OpenReview.

EXPLANATION: The paper "A Hierarchy of Graphical Models for Counterfactual Inferences" by Hongshuo Yang and Elias Bareinboim was published at NeurIPS 2025 and is confirmed in multiple sources including the Columbia CausalAI Laboratory repository and OpenReview.
LINK: https://causalai.net/r130.pdf
FOUND_TITLE: A Hierarchy of Graphical Models for Counterfactual Inferences
FOUND_AUTHORS: Hongshuo Yang, Elias Bareinboim
FOUND_YEAR: 2025
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
